page: Google Cloud Platform c2d-highcpu-16 is a compute-optimized virtual machine instance powered by the x86_64 AMD EPYC 7B13 processor. It features 16 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 8 physical cores with multithreading enabled. The instance is configured with 32 GB of system memory, yielding a compute-focused ratio of 2 GB of RAM per vCPU. It does not include local NVMe storage or GPU accelerators, relying entirely on network-attached storage.

Benchmark evaluations indicate strong single-core CPU performance, with stress-ng single-core tests placing in the top 25% of its class. Multi-core performance, measured via stress-ng and PassMark CPU tests, falls within the average range. Memory subsystem performance is robust, highlighted by a strong PassMark memory score, though uncached read and write memory bandwidth benchmarks remain average. The CPU supports advanced instruction sets including AVX, AES, VAES, and SHA_NI, which accelerate cryptographic operations and integer calculations.

Given its compute-heavy resource density, the c2d-highcpu-16 is highly cost-efficient for workloads that require dedicated CPU cycles without massive memory overhead. It is well-suited for tasks such as software compilation, web serving, and lightweight LLM text generation using small models, where it demonstrates strong text generation speeds. However, the lack of local storage and a 2 GB per vCPU memory limit represent tradeoffs for memory-intensive databases or large-scale data processing pipelines.
description: Google Cloud Platform c2d-highcpu-16 is an x86_64 compute-optimized instance equipped with 16 dedicated vCPUs and 32 GB of RAM, powered by an AMD EPYC 7B13 processor. This configuration provides a 2 GB per vCPU memory ratio, making it highly efficient for compute-bound workloads that do not require massive memory footprints. The instance lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Performance benchmarks show strong single-core CPU execution and memory efficiency, alongside average multi-core and memory bandwidth capabilities. Supported CPU instruction sets include AVX, AES, and SHA_NI, which enhance cryptographic and mathematical operations. The c2d-highcpu-16 is suited for software compilation, web serving, and small-scale language model text generation.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c2d-highcpu-16 is an x86_64 compute-optimized instance featuring 16 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs, 32 GB of RAM, and strong single-core performance for compute-intensive workloads.

tagline: A dedicated AMD EPYC compute-optimized instance delivering strong single-core performance and balanced memory throughput for CPU-bound workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by an AMD EPYC 7B13 processor with 16 dedicated vCPUs and 8 physical cores
Equipped with 32 GB of RAM, providing a compute-optimized 2 GB per vCPU ratio
Delivers strong single-core CPU performance and high memory efficiency in benchmarks
Supports advanced instruction sets including AVX, AES, VAES, and SHA_NI
Best suited for software compilation, web serving, and small-scale LLM text generation
